question_text: Suppose that 𝑋= (𝑋1, … , 𝑋𝑝) 𝑇 follows 𝑁𝑝(0, Σ), where Σ is a positive definite matrix. Then which of the following statements is/are correct?

options.A: 𝑋1, … , 𝑋𝑝 are always independent normal random variables
options.B: 𝑋1, … , 𝑋𝑝 are normal random variables
options.C: 2 + ⋯+ 𝑋𝑝 2 always follows a chi-square distribution
options.D: Any linear combination of 𝑋1, … , 𝑋𝑝 is a normal random variable
